summary: Recent updates to the 2D graphics rendering in Firefox focus on improving the way fonts are handled within the browser. Enhancements include changes to how font entries are created and managed, ensuring safer memory handling by using reference-counting to prevent errors related to font deletion. Additionally, there is added support for complex font features such as validating the length of kern subtables (used for spacing between characters) and accommodating specific character transformations involving the capital es-zet (U+1E9E). These changes are crucial as they help improve font rendering reliability and performance, ensuring that users experience smoother and more accurate text display across various websites. **By enhancing font management, Firefox ensures better visual consistency and less likelihood of rendering issues, leading to an overall more polished browsing experience.**
